content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
900 N. Palm Street
Turlock, California 95380
(209) 668-5579

ANGIL P. MORRIS
CITY ATTORNEY

Turlock

May 21, 1990

Mr. John Rothman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Conflicts of Interest - Mayor and Council Member

Dear Mr. Rothman:

Pursuant to our telephone conversation of May 18, 1990, I am hereby providing

you with additional information regarding the following two newly elected officials of the

City of Turlock:

Dr. Curt Andre - Elected Mayor in April, 1990. Dr. Andre is a

local, practicing optometrist within the City of

Turlock and one-hundred percent (100%)

shareholder in Andre, Inc. He conducts this

practice with Dr. Jack M. Makin, both receiving

a salary from the corporation. Is it a

disqualifying conflict for Dr. Andre to vote on

matters that concern his patients? What is your

opinion if he were to transfer all patients who

appear before the council to Dr. Makin? What

Post Office Box T • Turlock, California 95381-1526

I can see this appears to be a mostly blank document with a circular stamp or watermark visible in the upper portion, but I cannot make out any clearly legible text to transcribe. The image quality and contrast make it difficult to discern specific written content beyond the watermark/stamp.

If there is specific text you'd like me to attempt to transcribe, please provide a clearer image or let me know which sections contain the content you're interested in.

is your opinion if the patients he sees have
insurance and, therefore, the insurance
company is the payor. In this regard, Dr.
Andre is a listed provider in a preferred
provider organization (PPO) and the patient has
merely selected him for care.

Carolyn Ratto        -    Elected Council Member in April, 1990. Mrs.
Ratto's husband is a sole practitioner of
dentistry within the City of Turlock. Mrs. Ratto
is uninformed of the identities of her husband's
entire case load of patients. Should one of her
husband's patients appear before the council,
would Mrs. Ratto have to disqualify herself from
hearing the matter? For example, one of the
projects that is coming before the City Council
is Foster Farms prezoning application. Dr.
Ratto has patients that employees of Foster
Farms. Foster Farms is a self-insured employer.
However, Foster Farms does not refer
employees to Dr. Ratto, the employees can
choose any doctor they choose.

As I explained to you, I am seeking a more definitive interpretation of how the
commission applies California Government Code § 87103(c) and (d) as it relates to the
foregoing situations. I appreciate your kind assistance in this matter. Should you
require any more information, please feel free to contact me.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

[signature]
ANGIL P. MORRIS, City Attorney

APM:lk

embedding.qa_text: May 21, 1990 Mr. John Rothman Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Re: Conflicts of Interest - Mayor and Council Member Dear Mr. Rothman: Pursuant to our telephone conversation of May 18, 1990, I am hereby providing you with additional information regarding the following two newly elected officials of the City of Turlock: Dr. Curt Andre - Elected Mayor in April, 1990. Dr. Andre is a local, practicing optometrist within the City of Turlock and one-hundred percent (100%) shareholder in Andre, Inc. He conducts this practice with Dr. Jack M. Makin, both receiving a salary from the corporation. Is it a disqualifying conflict for Dr. Andre to vote on matters that concern his patients? What is your opinion if he were to transfer all patients who appear before the council to Dr. Makin? What The image is completely white, with no visible text or content to transcribe. It appears to be an empty page or a blank sheet of paper. is your opinion if the patients he sees have insurance and, therefore, the insurance company is the payor. In this regard, Dr. Andre is a listed provider in a preferred provider organization (PPO) and the patient has merely selected him for care. Carolyn Ratto - Elected Council Member in April, 1990. Mrs. Ratto’s husband is a sole practitioner of dentistry within the City of Turlock. Mrs. Ratto is uninformed of the identities of her husband’s entire case load of patients. Should one of her husband’s patients appear before the council, would Mrs. Ratto have to disqualify herself from hearing the matter? For example, one of the projects that is coming before the City Council is Foster Farms prezoning application. Dr. Ratto has patients that employees of Foster Farms. Foster Farms is a self-insured employer. However, Foster Farms does not refer employees to Dr. Ratto, the employees can choose any doctor they choose. As I explained to you, I am seeking a more definitive interpretation of how the commission applies California Government Code § 87103(c) and (d) as it relates to the foregoing situations. I appreciate your kind assistance in this matter. Should you require any more information, please feel free to contact me. Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in this matter. Sincerely, ANGIL P. MORRIS, City Attorney APM:lk
